creator: bob@eager.cx
text:
The author states quite strongly that GCC should be used. Although I
was able to compile it with CLANG, I wasn't comfortable because the
fact that it compiles doesn't mean there isn't some subtle error when
using CLANG (one of my own ports compiles nicely on both, yet fails
with CLANG because some valid statements are optimised out). Not being
the original author, I don't have the same facilities for regression
testing that he does. I will try to contact him and discuss this,
though; I know it's a pain needing GCC.

I didn't feel the debug options were useful for an end user, only for the author, and wanted to keep it simple. It's a while since I did that part,
and I seem to remember some minor issue as well.
I will look at that once it is committed, and try to fix it.

With regard to the minor issue with HELP, I'll look at that and issue a minor patch soon (working on one of my own ports right now, after an upstream version
bump).

Thanks!
